claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 4 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

The first thing I need to know is about the child you're claiming for:

**How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?**

Please let me know if they are:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi there! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm actually asking about my three children - I have Alex who's 7, Blake who's 12, and Charlie who's 10. So they're all under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know about your three children! That's helpful information.

Since **Alex (7)**, **Blake (12)**, and **Charlie (10)** are all under 16 years old, they all automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. This is great news!

Now let me check the next requirement for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
The next question relates to whether the children are working:

**Are all three of your children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12), I'd expect they're not in regular paid employment, but I just need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them work at all - they're too young for that! They're all just in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Since Alex, Blake, and Charlie are all in school and not working, they meet this requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Are all three children NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This refers to benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that a young person might claim themselves. Given their ages, it's very unlikely, but I need to confirm:

Are Alex, Blake, and Charlie all free from claiming any such benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them get any benefits in their own names. They're just kids!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly, that's what I expected! Let's continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are any of your children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie) currently on an apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (7, 10, and 12) and that they're all in school, I'd assume the answer is no, but I just need to confirm that none of them are in any kind of apprenticeship programme.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - they're way too young for apprenticeships! They're all just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! Primary school children wouldn't be on apprenticeships. Let's move on to the next part of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **ANY** of these apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself (and not claiming certain benefits like Income Support or Universal Credit)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these situations apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at all - I'm abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since you're living abroad, let me ask a follow-up question:

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include people working for the UK government overseas, such as:
- Diplomats
- Members of the armed forces stationed abroad
- Other government employees posted overseas

Also, do you have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme (settled or pre-settled status) that might apply, even while living abroad?

If none of these apply to you, unfortunately this may affect your eligibility for Child Benefit, as it generally requires you to be living in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances I mentioned.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I don't have any EU Settlement Scheme status either - I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being clear about your situation. Unfortunately, I have to deliver some disappointing news.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough to give you my assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Result

### ❌ Not Eligible

I'm sorry to say that based on your current circumstances, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** for any of your three children.

---

### Summary for Each Child:

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 7 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 12 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Charlie** | 10 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

---

### Why?

While all three of your children meet the requirements to be "qualifying children" (they're under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not on apprenticeships), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements** needed to claim Child Benefit.

To be eligible, the claimant must:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Have settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with certain conditions), **OR**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

---

### What Can You Do?

- **If your circumstances change** and you return to live in the UK, you may become eligible at that point
- **If someone else in the UK** is responsible for your children (such as a partner who lives in the UK), they may be able to claim instead
- For more information, visit: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility

I'm sorry I couldn't give you better news. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex (age 7) meets all the child-related requirements: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not on an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, nor do they have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

Blake (age 12) meets all the child-related requirements: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not on an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, nor do they have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

Charlie (age 10) meets all the child-related requirements: under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not on an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements as they are living abroad and are not a Crown servant, nor do they have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.
arguments.overall_reasoning: While all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) meet the qualifying child requirements for Child Benefit, the claimant is not eligible because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any of the special circumstances: they are not a Crown servant, and they do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
